evidence_excerpt: Over the 20-month period, DHA was safe and well-tolerated; both showed an overall stabilization of clinical symptoms, rather than the expected deterioration.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Persistent neurologic symptoms (ataxia, dysarthria, limb weakness) with mild changes (e.g., mild dexterity deterioration) and unchanged EQ-5D-3L scores suggest ongoing but limited-impact symptom burden.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: At 6 months, P2’s 6MWT increased from 400 m at T0 to 475 m (and 10MWT improved to 9.2 s).
At 12 months, P1’s SARA rose to 11.5 (from 7 at T0), indicating interim worsening.
At 20 months, both showed mild improvement in lower limb functions (DSI-ARSACS items 5–7).
At 20 months, P2 had mild improvement in speech (SARA item 4; DSI-ARSACS item 1).
At 20 months, both had mild deterioration of right limb dexterity (SARA items 5 and 7/8; DSI-ARSACS item 3).
At 20 months, P1’s LDL (241 mg/dL) and total cholesterol (282 mg/dL) were markedly elevated; both patients interrupted DHA due to cardiovascular risk concerns.

notes_on_uncertainty: This case report describes two siblings; outcomes are summarized across both since the dominant long-term pattern (stabilization) was similar. The reference time approximates the pre-treatment baseline evaluation (T0). Follow-up beyond 20 months is not documented.
